cases.description: Equivalent raw seams with the same owner, seam kind, flow sink, discriminator, and assertion shape remain one canonical group.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Equivalent raw seams can share one canonical gap identity.
must_not_claim: Do not collapse different missing discriminators into this group.
Do not use raw line number as the canonical identity.

cases.description: The same match arm keeps canonical identity across line movement while distinct arms stay separated.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Same match-arm discriminator keeps canonical identity across line movement.
must_not_claim: Do not group a different match arm with this identity.
Do not use a generic match or => discriminator.

cases.description: A same-file lexical match must not outrank a direct owner call with activation overlap.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Direct owner calls with activation overlap should be preferred over weak lexical affinity.
must_not_claim: Do not call a same-file lexical hit high-confidence without direct evidence.
Do not let changed-test recency outrank owner-call evidence.

cases.description: Exact error variant assertions are stronger than broad is_err style oracles.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Exact error variant evidence observes the changed variant more specifically than broad error checks.
must_not_claim: Do not treat is_err as exact variant discrimination.
Do not upgrade broad error oracles without variant evidence.

cases.description: A test that computes expected output through the owner under test is not independent exact-value evidence.
cases.fixture_reference: future benchmark fixture: self-computed expected value

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Self-computed expected values remain weak until expected data is independent.
must_not_claim: Do not mark self-computed expected values as strong exact-value evidence.
Do not claim equivalent code proves discriminator coverage.

cases.description: A duplicated equality assertion is not independent exact-value evidence.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Duplicated equality stays weak because it observes only a tautological relationship.
must_not_claim: Do not mark assert_eq!(actual, actual) as strong exact-value evidence.
Do not remove the upgrade suggestion for an independent expected value.

cases.description: Opaque assertion helpers remain static limitations until a supported helper pattern is added.
cases.expected_claim: Opaque helpers are analyzer limitations, not focused user test gaps.
must_not_claim: Do not infer exact-value semantics from an unsupported helper name.
Do not promote this class without positive and negative helper fixtures.

cases.description: Cross-file constants that cannot be resolved remain static limitations.
cases.fixture_reference: future benchmark fixture: cross-file constant boundary

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Unresolved cross-file constants stay visible as analyzer limits.
must_not_claim: Do not invent candidate values from unresolved constants.
Do not treat unresolved constants as missing user assertions.

cases.description: Same-test struct literal fields passed into the owner call are concrete activation values when the field values are literal.

sample.source:
let case = DiscountCase { amount: 100, threshold: 100 };
assert_eq!(discounted_total(case.amount, case.threshold), 90);

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Literal same-test struct fields can resolve activation values for field-projection owner call arguments while preserving missing-discriminator repair routing.
must_not_claim: Do not treat resolved activation values as proof that a missing boundary discriminator is checked.
Do not claim strong grip from activation-value resolution alone.
Do not resolve helper-returned struct projections.
Do not resolve non-literal struct fields.
Do not add cross-file or semantic struct analysis.

cases.description: A same-test struct literal field projection remains a concrete activation value when the owner call happens before a later shadow of the binding.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Source-order-scoped same-test struct literal fields can resolve activation values for owner calls before a later shadow while preserving stale-value guards after the shadow.
must_not_claim: Do not reuse stale literal fields for owner calls at or after the shadowing line.
Do not resolve literal field projections before the binding appears.
Do not resolve helper-returned struct projections.
Do not infer semantic struct flow outside the same test body.
Do not claim strong grip from activation-value resolution alone.

cases.description: A direct no-argument owner call activates value-insensitive seams without inventing synthetic activation values.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Direct no-argument owner calls can satisfy activation for value-insensitive seams without requiring concrete activation values.
must_not_claim: Do not invent synthetic observed values for no-argument calls.
Do not apply the no-argument activation route to predicate-boundary value checks.
Do not treat same-file, owner-named, or fixture-affinity relations as activation without a direct owner call.

cases.description: A multiline direct no-argument owner call activates value-insensitive seams without requiring same-line argument parsing.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Multiline direct no-argument owner calls can satisfy activation for value-insensitive seams without requiring concrete activation values or same-line argument parsing.
must_not_claim: Do not invent synthetic observed values for multiline no-argument calls.
Do not apply this route to predicate-boundary value checks.
Do not treat assertion-target affinity or owner-name mentions as activation without a direct owner call.
Do not treat macro invocations with the same name as direct function owner calls.

cases.description: A call-presence seam whose call expression only shares a generic argument token with an unrelated assertion must not gain assertion-target affinity.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Generic call argument tokens such as path are insufficient to relate a test to a call-presence seam; RIPR should keep raw findings as evidence instead of manufacturing an affinity-only activation limitation.
must_not_claim: Do not treat generic argument tokens such as path, value, text, line, or file as call-presence assertion-target affinity.
Do not turn generic token affinity into activation for value-insensitive call-presence seams.
Do not drop specific call-target tokens such as zq_quote_target_token or zq_render_target_token when an assertion actually mentions them.

cases.description: A same-file one-hop helper that directly calls the owner activates value-insensitive seams without inventing observed values.

sample.source:
fn exercise_device_labels() -> Vec<&'static str> { device_labels() }
#[test] fn helper_reaches_device_labels() { let labels = exercise_device_labels(); assert!(labels.is_empty()); }

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: A one-hop same-file helper that directly calls the owner can satisfy activation for value-insensitive seams without requiring concrete activation values.
must_not_claim: Do not follow multi-hop helpers.
Do not use a helper unless it is in the same file as the test and directly calls the owner.
Do not invent synthetic observed values for helper owner calls.
Do not apply this route to predicate-boundary value checks.
Do not treat helper name affinity alone as activation without a direct owner call inside the helper.

cases.description: A same-file helper whose name mentions the owner but does not call the owner remains a named activation-owner-call limitation.

sample.source:
fn exercise_device_labels() -> Vec<&'static str> { Vec::new() }
#[test] fn return_value_contract_mentions_empty_output() { let return_value = exercise_device_labels(); assert!(return_value.is_empty()); }

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Helper name affinity without a direct owner call remains a named static limitation, not activation or user test debt.
must_not_claim: Do not treat helper name affinity alone as activation.
Do not treat a helper-local Vec::new return as activation for the production owner.
Do not invent observed values from helper return values.
Do not classify helper-name-only value-insensitive seams as user test debt.

cases.description: A direct owner call with opaque arguments activates value-insensitive seams without inventing synthetic activation values.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Direct owner calls can satisfy activation for value-insensitive seams even when argument values remain opaque, without creating synthetic value facts.
must_not_claim: Do not invent synthetic observed values for opaque owner-call arguments.
Do not apply this route to predicate-boundary value checks.
Do not treat same-file, owner-named, import-affinity, or fixture-affinity relations as activation without a direct owner call.
Do not claim strong grip from value-insensitive activation alone.

cases.description: A value-insensitive return seam with only assertion-target affinity remains a static limitation, but the limitation names the missing direct owner-call path instead of value-resolution debt.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Affinity-only related tests for value-insensitive seams remain named static limitations until RIPR sees a direct owner call or stronger reach proof.
must_not_claim: Do not treat assertion-target affinity as activation without a direct owner call.
Do not invent observed values for value-insensitive seams.
Do not classify affinity-only value-insensitive seams as user test debt.
Do not apply this owner-call limitation route to predicate-boundary value checks.

cases.description: A field projection from a helper-built fixture remains unresolved because the struct field values are not visible in the same test body.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Helper-built struct projections remain named static limitations until analyzer support can inspect the helper safely.
must_not_claim: Do not invent candidate values from helper-returned structs.
Do not treat opaque fixture helpers as observed activation values.
Do not claim semantic field resolution outside the same test body.

cases.description: A same-test struct literal field projection remains unresolved when the binding is shadowed before the owner call.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Shadowed struct literal projections remain named static limitations because the earlier literal value is stale by the owner call.
must_not_claim: Do not reuse stale literal fields after a binding is shadowed before the owner call.
Do not treat a before-call shadow as an after-call shadow.
Do not treat shadowed helper-built projections as observed activation values.

cases.description: A fixture/rstest parameter field projection remains unresolved even if a later same-name struct literal appears in the test body.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Fixture parameter projections remain named static limitations because a later literal binding is not proof of the runtime fixture value.
must_not_claim: Do not resolve fixture/rstest parameter fields from later same-name literals.
Do not let source-order struct projection support override fixture/rstest parameter invalidation.
Do not treat runtime fixture parameters as same-test literal struct fields.

cases.description: A same-test struct literal field projection remains unresolved when a loop or pattern binder shadows the struct literal before the owner call.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Struct projections shadowed by loop or pattern binders before the owner call remain named static limitations because the earlier literal value is stale by the owner call.
must_not_claim: Do not reuse stale literal fields after for/if-let/closure/match binders shadow the identifier.
Do not treat a before-call binder shadow as an after-call shadow.
Do not treat shadowed helper-built projections as observed activation values.

cases.description: A same-test struct literal field projection remains unresolved when a non-simple let pattern shadows the struct literal before the owner call.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

cases.expected_claim: Struct projections shadowed by non-simple let patterns before the owner call remain named static limitations because the earlier literal value is stale by the owner call.
must_not_claim: Do not reuse stale literal fields after let-pattern binders shadow the identifier.
Do not treat a before-call let-pattern shadow as an after-call shadow.
Do not treat pattern-bound helper projections as observed activation values.

cases.description: A changed label/help-text constant should become one presentation-text evidence item with visibility and actionability, not two raw line-local actions.
cases.fixture_reference: screenshot-derived benchmark: APPLE_M3_AIR_DEVICE_LABELS_TEXT declaration plus string literal

sample.source:
pub const APPLE_M3_AIR_DEVICE_LABELS_TEXT: &str =
    "apple-m3-air-cpu-neon = M3 MacBook Air Apple CPU/NEON lane";

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant

presentation_text.text_literal: apple-m3-air-cpu-neon = M3 MacBook Air Apple CPU/NEON lane
presentation_text.suggested_assertion: Trace the constant to a supported output sink before adding or updating tests.

cases.expected_claim: Changed presentation text is only actionable when RIPR can identify a plausible observable output path or observer.
must_not_claim: Do not create user test debt from text alone.
Do not recommend mutation testing as the first action for a visibility-unknown text label.
Do not produce independent actions for the declaration line and string literal line.
Do not treat internal labels as user-visible presentation text.
Do not infer user visibility through opaque helpers.

cases.description: User-visible output text with no supported observer becomes one actionable canonical item, not a raw finding count.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed text flows to CLI help output and no supported output observer is found.
evidence_record.recommended_repair: Add or update a help-output snapshot assertion for HELP_DEVICE_LABEL.

presentation_text.suggested_assertion: Assert CLI help output includes the HELP_DEVICE_LABEL text.

cases.expected_claim: A visible unobserved text change can become one actionable output-observer repair.
must_not_claim: Do not count the declaration and literal as two user actions.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
Do not call lexical-only tests observers.

cases.description: User-visible output text with a supported snapshot or golden observer becomes an already-observed no-action item.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed report text flows to rendered output and a golden observer covers it.

presentation_text.suggested_assertion: Existing golden observer already covers the rendered report output.

cases.expected_claim: A visible observed text change remains visible as evidence but does not become a user repair.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable gap when a supported output observer exists.
Do not hide the raw findings.
Do not treat golden-observed text as unobserved snapshot debt.

cases.description: Likely user-visible presentation text with an unsupported observer topology remains an observer-unknown limitation instead of becoming user test debt.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed text appears to reach rendered help output, but the observer path is hidden behind an unsupported helper.
evidence_record.recommended_repair: Trace the rendered help output to a supported snapshot, help-output, report, table, or golden observer before claiming a user repair.

presentation_text.suggested_assertion: Trace the rendered help output to a supported observer before adding or updating tests.

cases.expected_claim: Likely visible text with unsupported observer topology stays a named limitation until observer evidence is fixture-backed.
must_not_claim: Do not call observer-unknown text an actionable output-observer gap.
Do not infer observer coverage from helper names alone.
Do not recommend mutation testing before tracing the output observer.
Do not suppress the raw declaration and literal findings.

cases.description: Internal-only labels become canonical no-action evidence items instead of user-visible test debt.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed label is confined to an internal proof-lane map in fixture-backed scope.

presentation_text.suggested_assertion: No user-facing assertion is recommended for this internal label.

cases.expected_claim: Internal-only text changes are explicit no-action items, not suppressed raw evidence.
must_not_claim: Do not treat internal labels as user-visible behavior.
Do not recommend snapshot tests for internal-only labels.
Do not suppress raw findings without preserving supporting evidence.

cases.description: The same presentation-text constant keeps canonical identity when declaration and literal lines move together.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant

cases.expected_claim: Presentation-text canonical identity is stable across declaration/literal line movement.
must_not_claim: Do not use raw line number as the presentation-text identity.
Do not split declaration and literal movement into separate user actions.
Do not collide with a different constant that has the same literal text.

cases.description: Two different presentation-text constants with similar text must remain separate canonical items.
cases.fixture_reference: RIPR-SPEC-0045 unrelated constants do not group

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: constant_owner_identity

cases.expected_claim: Grouping uses owner identity and evidence class, not similar literal text alone.
must_not_claim: Do not merge different constants because their text is similar.
Do not let one constant's observer satisfy a different constant.
Do not treat a shared token as canonical identity.

cases.description: Internal policy metadata aligns into one canonical no-action item instead of user-visible test debt.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed constant is confined to internal allowlist policy metadata in fixture-backed scope.

config_policy.suggested_assertion: No user-facing assertion is recommended for this internal policy constant.

cases.expected_claim: Internal policy metadata remains countable evidence but does not become user repair work.
must_not_claim: Do not create user test debt from internal policy metadata alone.
Do not recommend output observers for internal-only constants.
Do not hide the raw declaration and literal findings.

cases.description: A rendered config or policy label with no supported observer becomes one actionable output-observer item.
cases.fixture_reference: RIPR-SPEC-0048 rendered report/config label without observer

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed policy label flows to rendered report output and no supported observer is found.
evidence_record.recommended_repair: Add or update a report-render, config-output, snapshot, or golden observer for REPORT_POLICY_LABEL.

cases.expected_claim: A visible unobserved config or policy label can become one actionable output-observer repair.
must_not_claim: Do not count declaration and literal findings as separate user actions.
Do not recommend mutation testing before output-observer work.
Do not call lexical-only tests observers.

cases.description: A validation or routing selector with no behavior discriminator becomes one actionable behavior-discriminator item.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed validation selector flows to observable validation behavior and no supported discriminator is found.
evidence_record.recommended_repair: Add or update a behavior discriminator test for VALIDATION_SELECTOR.

config_policy.suggested_assertion: Assert the selected behavior includes the VALIDATION_SELECTOR value or selected behavior.

cases.expected_claim: A supported validation or routing selector can become one actionable behavior-discriminator repair.
must_not_claim: Do not treat behavior selectors as output labels.
Do not count declaration and literal findings as separate user actions.
Do not accept lexical-only tests as behavior discriminators.
Do not recommend mutation testing before behavior-discriminator work.

cases.description: A validation or routing selector with a supported behavior discriminator becomes an already-observed no-action item.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed validation selector reaches observable behavior and an existing behavior discriminator covers it.

config_policy.suggested_assertion: Existing validation behavior test already covers the selected behavior.

cases.expected_claim: A supported validation or routing discriminator keeps the behavior selector visible as already observed, not user repair work.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable behavior-discriminator gap when a supported behavior observer exists.
Do not hide the raw selector findings.
Do not let an unrelated validation test satisfy the selector.

cases.description: A schema field label observed by a supported schema-render or golden test becomes an already-observed no-action item.

evidence_record.why: Changed schema field label reaches rendered output and an existing golden observer covers it.

config_policy.suggested_assertion: Existing schema-render golden already covers the changed field label.

cases.expected_claim: Observed schema or report labels stay visible as evidence but do not become user repair work.
must_not_claim: Do not emit an actionable gap when a supported schema or golden observer exists.
Do not hide the raw findings.

cases.description: A config constant routed through untraceable cross-file formatting remains a named flow limitation instead of user test debt.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed config label may flow through cross-file rendering, but RIPR cannot trace the output sink in supported scope.
evidence_record.recommended_repair: Trace CONFIG_TABLE_LABEL to a supported output, schema, validation, or behavior sink before claiming user test debt.

config_policy.suggested_assertion: Trace the constant to a supported output or behavior sink before adding tests.

cases.expected_claim: Untraceable config flow stays a named analyzer limitation until sink evidence is supported.
must_not_claim: Do not create user test debt from untraceable config flow.
Do not infer user-visible output from cross-file formatting alone.
Do not recommend mutation testing as the first repair.

cases.description: A config label hidden behind an opaque lookup remains a named limitation and does not become an actionable observer gap.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: declaration_and_literal_same_text_constant
evidence_record.why: Changed config label is routed through an unsupported lookup helper.
evidence_record.recommended_repair: Add fixture-backed support for this lookup shape before claiming visibility or observer debt.

config_policy.suggested_assertion: Inspect the lookup path or add analyzer support before adding output-observer tests.

cases.expected_claim: Opaque config lookup stays a named limitation until lookup evidence is fixture-backed.
must_not_claim: Do not infer user-visible output from helper names alone.
Do not call an opaque lookup an actionable output-observer gap.

cases.description: A state, event, persistence, or metric observer can support side-effect evidence in checked fixture scope.

cases.expected_claim: Supported side-effect observers can make the observed side effect explicit.
must_not_claim: Do not treat any side-effect call as observed without an observer.

cases.description: A snapshot oracle with a known discriminating field is stronger than broad snapshot output.

must_not_claim: Do not treat broad snapshots as field-specific evidence.

cases.description: A mock expectation can observe a side-effect call when the expected target and argument are explicit.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape

must_not_claim: Do not treat unsupported mock shapes as observed side effects.

cases.description: Runtime-only evidence appears in calibration and must not create a static gap.
cases.expected_claim: Runtime-only signal is calibration evidence, not a static gap.
must_not_claim: Do not create seams from runtime-only calibration rows.

cases.description: Runtime outcomes that join to more than one static seam remain ambiguous.

evidence_record.canonical_gap_reason: same owner, seam kind, flow sink, missing discriminator, and assertion shape
